steps.title: Factor the polynomial
steps.content: Start by factoring out the greatest common factor: $$2x^3+42x^2+208x=2x(x^2+21x+104).$$ The quadratic factors because $8\cdot13=104$ and $8+13=21$: $$2x(x^2+21x+104)=2x(x+8)(x+13).$$

steps.title: Convert roots to factors
steps.content: The negative roots $-8$ and $-13$ correspond to factors $x+8$ and $x+13$. Therefore the possible positive values of $b$ are $8$ and $13$. The smallest possible value is $8$.
